acceptance_targets: [chapter_14_f001] Pass if a cold reader can quote or paraphrase at least one actually bad prepared line or transition from Cleveland and one from Pittsburgh or Detroit, and can point to a concrete audience consequence attached to each by reading snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:7, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:23, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:71, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:119. Fail if those passages still rely mainly on labels such as 'borrowed,' 'serviceable,' or 'killed it' without showing what Doug said.

[chapter_14_f002] Pass if, by reading the revised Detroit riff in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:93 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:117, a cold reader can hear Doug thinking out loud rather than delivering six consecutive finished lines: at least one turn visibly self-corrects or wanders before landing, and no more than one sentence reads like a standalone aphorism without nearby concrete travel detail. Fail if the passage still reads as a chain of polished one-liners.

[chapter_14_f003] Pass if the revised closing call still contains the admission that the material fails and the two-weeks deadline by reading snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:143 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:149, but adds one new operational consequence beyond the already-shown truth/listening dynamic and does not spend two separate metaphors paraphrasing the same lesson. Fail if the Detroit scene still lands its meaning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:115 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:119 and the call afterward keeps explaining that meaning again.
[length_chapter_14_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 5500 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can quote or paraphrase at least one actually bad prepared line or transition from Cleveland and one from Pittsburgh or Detroit, and can point to a concrete audience consequence attached to each by reading snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:7, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:23, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:71, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:119. Fail if those passages still rely mainly on labels such as 'borrowed,' 'serviceable,' or 'killed it' without showing what Doug said.
findings.problem: The chapter keeps summarizing Doug's dead prepared act instead of making the reader sit through enough of the actual bad material. We are told he does 'borrowed comedy,' that the bits are merely 'present,' that the scripted opening is 'clean' and 'serviceable,' and that 'the reaching killed it,' but the embarrassing onstage content itself mostly stays off-page. In a comedy-ordeal chapter, that is sanitization by summary: the prose protects both writer and reader from the ugliest version of Doug's failure, which weakens the contrast when accidental truth finally works.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:7 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:9, replace some summary with 1-2 concrete borrowed lines or transitions that die in Cleveland. At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:23 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:25, let the reader hear the actual replacement-cost turn or another scripted connective that loses Pittsburgh. At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:71 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:119, render a short fragment of the prepared Detroit material before and after the live riff so the failure mode is audible, not just diagnosed.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, by reading the revised Detroit riff in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:93 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:117, a cold reader can hear Doug thinking out loud rather than delivering six consecutive finished lines: at least one turn visibly self-corrects or wanders before landing, and no more than one sentence reads like a standalone aphorism without nearby concrete travel detail. Fail if the passage still reads as a chain of polished one-liners.
findings.problem: Doug's Detroit breakthrough is effective, but too many consecutive lines arrive as polished, detachable comic insights rather than speech produced under panic and discovery. 'Pauses with vending machines,' 'the parking lot from a second location,' 'nobody is there on purpose,' and 'nobody knows you're there' all land cleanly enough that the set starts sounding composed. That drifts from Doug's style-bible register, which is flat, literal, and only accidentally incisive, and it triggers the book's warning against literary polish and aphoristic dialogue.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the road/hotel material at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:93, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:101, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:105, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:109, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:113, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:117, but roughen the run so Doug sounds like he is discovering the bit in real time. Add at least one false start, self-correction, or over-specific dead-end detail, and roughen or cut the cleanest quotable lines so the monologue feels overheard rather than workshop-perfect.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised closing call still contains the admission that the material fails and the two-weeks deadline by reading snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:143 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:149, but adds one new operational consequence beyond the already-shown truth/listening dynamic and does not spend two separate metaphors paraphrasing the same lesson. Fail if the Detroit scene still lands its meaning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:115 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:119 and the call afterward keeps explaining that meaning again.
findings.problem: The chapter already makes its turn legible when Detroit stops laughing and starts listening, then goes polite again the moment Doug reaches for the set list. The exit call keeps re-proving the same point in fresher wording: 'Like they wanted the report, not the brochure' and 'The set list's a stack of forms.' Those lines are smart, but they explain a truth the scene has already shown, so the ending loses pressure by paraphrasing its own meaning instead of moving fully into the unsolved next problem.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:135, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:139, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:143, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:147, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:149, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:153, compress the phone call so it keeps the must-land beats but stops restating why Detroit worked. Preserve Doug's admission that the built material fails, Simone's 'I know,' and the two-weeks-to-taping pressure; then pivot the exchange toward the operational problem that the alive version still cannot be summoned on purpose.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 5500 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's greatest risk is timidity. The premise — a man who is not funny trying to be funny — invites a safe, gently comic execution where Doug is lovably bumbling and everything works out. Resist this. Doug's failures should be genuinely uncomfortable to witness. His loneliness should be genuinely lonely, not charmingly solitary. The toast should contain things that are genuinely inappropriate, not safely edgy. The comedy should come from truth that hurts, not from situations that are merely awkward. The moments of tenderness — the Gloria chapter, the Kelsey lunch, the Simone conversation — should be earned through accumulated specificity, not through the narrative signaling that it's time to feel. Every chapter should contain at least one moment that a cautious writer would soften, and the drafting agent should not soften it.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism is a craft failure. If Doug is drunk, describe what drunk looks and feels like — the specific sensory experience, not the label. If Doug is lonely, show the loneliness through the objects and routines that fill the space where other people would be — the cereal dinner, the second bedroom, the unpacked boxes. If a scene involves embarrassment, shame, failure, or longing, render it in full physical and emotional detail. The reader should feel it, not be told about it. Replace every euphemism with the specific thing it is hiding.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for most characters. Uncontracted forms should be reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific moments (Doug going precise under stress, Megan's composed register). If a line reads stiff and the character is not being deliberately formal, contract it.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should share the same structural rhythm. Vary: scene count (1-4), dialogue density (high-dialogue chapters followed by interior-heavy chapters), pacing (frenetic chapters followed by still ones), emotional register (comic escalation followed by tender quiet), narrative distance (very close interior followed by observed social scenes). The novel should feel like it breathes — tight chapters followed by loose ones, crowded chapters followed by solitary ones. Chapter 3 (quiet office day) must feel structurally different from Chapter 10 (quiet visit to Gloria). Chapter 9 (the corporate disaster) must feel structurally different from Chapter 14 (the tour), even though both involve performance failure. Achieve variety through scene construction, not through gimmick.
prose_style_profile.diction: Mid-register, clean, precise. No pyrotechnics. No extended metaphors. The prose should have the quality of someone describing what happened accurately — not artlessly, but without reaching for beauty. When beauty arrives, it should feel accidental, like Doug's humor: a precisely observed detail that turns out to be devastating not because the prose reached for devastation but because the detail was exact. Insurance language may surface in narration when it captures something emotional more precisely than emotional language would. Avoid lyrical flights, extended similes, or any construction that would sound wrong coming from a narrator who is sympathetic to Doug's plainness.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, tightly bound to Doug's perspective. The narrator has access to Doug's interior — his observations, his assessments, his emotional processing through objects and details — but not to any other character's thoughts. Other characters are rendered through Doug's perception: what they say, how they move, what Doug notices about them. The narrator's voice is distinct from Doug's voice but sympathetic to it — slightly more articulate, slightly more aware, but never mocking and never superior. The humor in the narration comes from the same source as Doug's humor: precision about mundane things that reveals the absurdity or sadness underneath. The narrator does not explain the comedy. The narrator does not wink.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and controlled. Default to mid-length sentences with clean syntax. Use short sentences for comic timing — the flat declarative that follows an elaborate setup. Use longer, more syntactically complex sentences for interior passages where Doug is processing something he cannot name. Avoid uniform sentence length across paragraphs. The prose should breathe — alternating between compression and expansion, between observation and reflection, between the external world and Doug's interior. Comedy requires rhythm more than any other mode: the setup needs room, the punch needs precision, and the space after needs silence.
